caption: All-out attack (#689): a new order stance that trades defense for offense (Battle.OrderMode.ALL_OUT_ATTACK, hotkey ,). UnitCombat.order_mode_modifiers gives the attacking side +20% effective attack when the STRIKER is in this stance, and -20% effective defense when the TARGET is -- so a unit fighting all-out both hits harder and gets hit harder. This clip stages two matched Infantry regiments (identical stats, 100 soldiers each) head-on: the player unit (top) is selected and switched to All-out attack (,) the instant the clip starts, before the enemy closes -- the HUD flashes 'All-out attack' and the label appears over the unit. The two lines then clash under only that one asymmetry. State-dump verified against an identical un-modified baseline fight: by tick 300 the all-out unit has inflicted 12 casualties on the enemy (vs. 9 in the unmodified baseline) while itself taking 8 (vs. 7 baseline) -- it deals noticeably more damage than a fair fight would produce, at the cost of noticeably more of its own losses too.
